title: Smoky Italian Macaroni Bake
description: A smoky, fluffy, and comforting Polish-inspired dish featuring macaroni, perfect for a sick day, and low-carb-friendly. This recipe combines the richness of Italian flavors with the heartiness of Polish cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the macaroni according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato sauce to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked macaroni, meat sauce, and mozzarella cheese.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top with additional mozzarella cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
